description: A nylon pouch for a single rifle magazine.  Will fit many, though not all, rifle magazines.

variants.description: A nylon pouch fit for a single STANAG magazine.  When stored, 30-round standard magazines poke out of the top, where it's secured with an elastic band.  This pouch design makes it that much faster to use than a closed-top design with a flap.  Has PALS webbing on the front, fit for the same pouch or something smaller.  <variant:multicam>

A nylon pouch fit for a single STANAG magazine.  When stored, 30-round standard magazines poke out of the top, where it's secured with an elastic band.  This pouch design makes it that much faster to use than a closed-top design with a flap.  Has PALS webbing on the front, fit for the same pouch or something smaller.  <variant:black>

A nylon pouch fit for a single STANAG magazine.  When stored, 30-round standard magazines poke out of the top, where it's secured with an elastic band.  This pouch design makes it that much faster to use than a closed-top design with a flap.  Has PALS webbing on the front, fit for the same pouch or something smaller.  <variant:coyote>

A nylon pouch fit for a single STANAG magazine.  When stored, 30-round standard magazines poke out of the top, where it's secured with an elastic band.  This pouch design makes it that much faster to use than a closed-top design with a flap.  Has PALS webbing on the front, fit for the same pouch or something smaller.  <variant:ranger>

description: A nylon pouch fit for a single STANAG magazine and a single pistol magazine.  Not as quick to use as two separate pouches, but takes up fewer slots on the webbing.

variants.description: A nylon pouch fit for a single STANAG magazine and a single pistol magazine.  When stored, standard-length magazines poke out of the top.  Rifle magazines are secured with an elastic band, while pistol magazines are covered with a flap.  This design trades some quickness of use for covenience, since it allows you to carry magazines for both your rifle and your backup pistol in less space than two separate pouches would take.  <variant:multicam>

A nylon pouch fit for a single STANAG magazine and a single pistol magazine.  When stored, standard-length magazines poke out of the top.  Rifle magazines are secured with an elastic band, while pistol magazines are covered with a flap.  This design trades some quickness of use for covenience, since it allows you to carry magazines for both your rifle and your backup pistol in less space than two separate pouches would take.  <variant:black>

This design trades some quickness of use for covenience, since it allows you to carry magazines for both your rifle and your backup pistol in less space than two separate pouches would take.  <variant:coyote>

description: A long nylon pouch, intended to hold up to 12 shotgun shells.  The flap keeps the shells shielded from the elements.

variants.description: A long nylon pouch, intended to hold up to 12 shotgun shells, or ammunition of similar calibers.  The flap keeps the shells shielded from the elements.  There is a strip of hook-and-loop surface, for sticking morale patches onto.  <variant:multicam>

A long nylon pouch, intended to hold up to 12 shotgun shells, or ammunition of similar calibers.  The flap keeps the shells shielded from the elements.  There is a strip of hook-and-loop surface, for sticking morale patches onto.  <variant:black>

A long nylon pouch, intended to hold up to 12 shotgun shells, or ammunition of similar calibers.  The flap keeps the shells shielded from the elements.  There is a strip of hook-and-loop surface, for sticking morale patches onto.  <variant:coyote>

description: A set of six rifle-magazine pouches in a single pouch.  Named so because it has two rows of pouches stacked in front of the other.  The front row is quicker to use, but doesn't hold larger magazines.

variants.description: A set of six sewn-together STANAG magazine pouches.  The front row is noticably shorter, so while these pouches are quicker to draw from, they don't allow larger-than-standard magazines in.  Each front pouch has its own small PALS webbing.  <variant:multicam>

A set of six sewn-together STANAG magazine pouches.  The front row is noticably shorter, so while these pouches are quicker to draw from, they don't allow larger-than-standard magazines in.  Each front pouch has its own small PALS webbing.  <variant:black>

A set of six sewn-together STANAG magazine pouches.  The front row is noticably shorter, so while these pouches are quicker to draw from, they don't allow larger-than-standard magazines in.  Each front pouch has its own small PALS webbing.  <variant:coyote>

A set of six sewn-together STANAG magazine pouches.  The front row is noticably shorter, so while these pouches are quicker to draw from, they don't allow larger-than-standard magazines in.  Each front pouch has its own small PALS webbing.  <variant:olive>

description: A thin nylon pouch for holding a single pistol magazine.  Being of open-top design, it's quicker to operate than those with flaps.

variants.description: A thin nylon pouch with a flexible retention cord.  It is quicker to operate than those pouches that close with a flap.  Can store a single pistol magazine, including some of the extended ones.  <variant:multicam>

A thin nylon pouch with a flexible retention cord.  It is quicker to operate than those pouches that close with a flap.  Can store a single pistol magazine, including some of the extended ones.  <variant:coyote>

description: A nylon pouch fit for a pistol magazine.  The flap keeps the magazine soundly in place, but operating it is slower than the open-top alternatives.

name.str: MOLLE II older pistol mag pouch
name.str_pl: MOLLE II older pistol mag pouches

variants.description: An older, faded nylon pouch.  Can store a single pistol magazine, originally intended for the previously standard-issue M9 magazines.  The snap button on the flap makes the pouch slower to operate than the newer hook-and-loop flap on the M17 magazine pouch.  <variant:ucp>

description: A nylon pouch with a hook-and-loop flap.  Can store a single pistol magazine.  A newer design for the newer standard-issue army pistol magazines.

variants.description: A thin nylon pouch with a hook-and-loop flap.  Can store a single pistol magazine.  Originally designed to hold magazines for M17, the standard-issue pistol in the U.S. Army before the Cataclysm.  The flap is quicker to operate than the previous snap-button design, but not as quick as the open-top alternatives.  <variant:multicam>

description: A nylon pouch for storing an SMG magazine.  Also fits extended SMG magazines.

variants.description: A thin nylon pouch for storing a single SMG magazine.  Fits most regular and extended SMG magazines.  <variant:multicam>
A thin nylon pouch for storing a single SMG magazine.  Fits most regular and extended SMG magazines.  <variant:black>
A thin nylon pouch for storing a single SMG magazine.  Fits most regular and extended SMG magazines.  <variant:ranger>
A thin nylon pouch for storing a single SMG magazine.  Fits most regular and extended SMG magazines.  <variant:coyote>
A thin nylon pouch for storing a single SMG magazine.  Fits most regular and extended SMG magazines.  <variant:grey>

description: A nylon pouch for storing a single large magazine.

variants.description: A nylon pouch for storing a single magazine in .308/7.62×51mm NATO caliber.  Fits magazines up to 30 rounds in capacity.  <variant:multicam>
A nylon pouch for storing a single magazine in .308/7.62×51mm NATO caliber.  Fits magazines up to 30 rounds in capacity.  <variant:coyote>
A nylon pouch for storing a single magazine in .308/7.62×51mm NATO caliber.  Fits magazines up to 30 rounds in capacity.  <variant:black>
A nylon pouch for storing a single magazine in .308/7.62×51mm NATO caliber.  Fits magazines up to 30 rounds in capacity.  <variant:ranger>

description: A nylon pouch for a single .338 sniper rifle magazine.  Can also hold some .308 and 7.62mm magazines.

variants.description: A small nylon pouch intended to hold a single .338 Lapua 5-round magazine, or a magazines of similar size.  Produced by Warrior Assault Systems.  The plastic buckle keeps the magazine securely in place, but also makes it somewhat slower to operate than open-top alternatives.  <variant:multicam>

A small nylon pouch intended to hold a single .338 Lapua 5-round magazine, or a magazines of similar size.  Produced by Warrior Assault Systems.  The plastic buckle keeps the magazine securely in place, but also makes it somewhat slower to operate than open-top alternatives.  <variant:coyote>

A small nylon pouch intended to hold a single .338 Lapua 5-round magazine, or a magazines of similar size.  Produced by Warrior Assault Systems.  The plastic buckle keeps the magazine securely in place, but also makes it somewhat slower to operate than open-top alternatives.  <variant:atacs_fg>
